methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze pollen data, including visualization, calculation of pollen season metrics, and modeling of environmental influences on pollen allergen potency (PAP).

steps.description: All the statistical analysis were carried out by using the R programming language, using the package Aerobiology for pollen data visualization.
steps.searchable_summary: use R and Aerobiology package for pollen data visualization

steps.description: The MPS for the period 2010-2015 was calculated with the 95% method, taking as start date the moment when the concentration for one pollen type reached 2.5% and taking as end date the moment when the concentration reached the 97.5%.
The number of days with pollen concentrations higher than 100 pollen grains/m3 (Betula) and 50 pollen grains/m3 (Poaceae) were quantified.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify days with high pollen concentrations

steps.description: Generalized Additive Models (GAM) were established to explain the influencing parameters in PAP using the package mgcv with the expansion itsadug.
steps.searchable_summary: establish GAM models for PAP using mgcv and itsadug

steps.description: To check for statistical differences between the PAP for Davos and Munich and calculations for Fig. S9, a Mann-Whitney U test was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Mann-Whitney U test for PAP differences
inputs: PAP data for Davos and Munich

methods.description: Procedure for collecting and analyzing pollen and allergen samples from specific locations to calculate Pollen Allergen Potency (PAP).

steps.description: Airborne pollen data from 2010 to 2015 were obtained from the pollen station of Davos-Wolfgang and Munich.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain airborne pollen data from stations

steps.description: Aeroallergens were daily sampled at the same locations with the same time interval as for pollen using a ChemVol 2400 high-volume cascade impactor.
After collection, samples were stored separately in polypropylene tubes at −80°C until further extraction.
The quantification of allergens was done with specific ELISA for Bet v 1 or Phl p 5.

methods.description: Development of explanatory models to understand the significance of various parameters on Pollen Allergen Potency (PAP) using Generalized Additive Models (GAM).

steps.description: The time spent in the anther before releasing the pollen grains was found to be significant according to the GAM model.
steps.searchable_summary: model significance of anther time using GAM

steps.description: The additive models explained that for Poaceae the PAP was higher at the beginning and at the end of the pollen season.
steps.searchable_summary: explain PAP variation for Poaceae using additive models

steps.description: For Betula, the GAM model explained 74.5% of the PAP for Munich and 66.1% for Davos.
steps.searchable_summary: explain PAP for Betula using GAM
